was the 2nd season of the [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership"), the top-tier [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") won the championship for the second consecutive season. African Stars player [Willy Stephanus](/wiki/Willy_Stephanus "Willy Stephanus") and [Rewaldo Prins](/wiki/Rewaldo_Prins "Rewaldo Prins") of [Khomas NAMPOL](/wiki/Khomas_NAMPOL_FC "Khomas NAMPOL FC") were the league's top scorers with sixteen goals each.[[1]](#cite_note-movement_abroad-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Stars_Back_to_Back_Champions-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league season

[Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Season | 2023–24 |
| Dates | 16 September 2023 – 27 April 2024 |
| Champions | [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") |
| Matches | 240 |
| Top goalscorer | [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") [Willy Stephanus](/wiki/Willy_Stephanus "Willy Stephanus")  [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") [Rewaldo Prins](/wiki/Rewaldo_Prins "Rewaldo Prins")  (16 goals each) |
| ← [2022–23](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Namibia_Premier_Football_League "2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League")  [2024–25](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_Namibia_Premiership "2024–25 Namibia Premiership") → | |

The **2023–24 Namibia Premiership** was the 2nd season of the [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership"), the top-tier [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") won the championship for the second consecutive season. African Stars player [Willy Stephanus](/wiki/Willy_Stephanus "Willy Stephanus") and [Rewaldo Prins](/wiki/Rewaldo_Prins "Rewaldo Prins") of [Khomas NAMPOL](/wiki/Khomas_NAMPOL_FC "Khomas NAMPOL FC") were the league's top scorers with sixteen goals each.[[1]](#cite_note-movement_abroad-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Stars_Back_to_Back_Champions-2)

The new clubs promoted for the season were: [Okakarara Young Warriors](/w/index.php?title=Okakarara_Young_Warriors&action=edit&redlink=1 "Okakarara Young Warriors (page does not exist)") from the Northwest division, [Eeshoke Chula Chula FC](/wiki/Eeshoke_Chula_Chula_FC "Eeshoke Chula Chula FC") from the North East division, and [Khomas NAMPOL FC](/wiki/Khomas_NAMPOL_FC "Khomas NAMPOL FC") from the Southern region. Tura Magic FC were renamed [Ongos Valley FC](/wiki/Ongos_Valley_FC "Ongos Valley FC") prior to the start of the season.[[3]](#cite_note-Ongos_Valley-3)

Following the season, [Orlando Pirates](/wiki/Orlando_Pirates_S.C. "Orlando Pirates S.C.") were relegated for the first time in club history. [Life Fighters](/wiki/Life_Fighters_F.C. "Life Fighters F.C.") and newly-promoted [Okakarara Young Warriors](/w/index.php?title=Okakarara_Young_Warriors&action=edit&redlink=1 "Okakarara Young Warriors (page does not exist)") were also relegated.[[2]](#cite_note-Stars_Back_to_Back_Champions-2)
